content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56@ o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 6,2004
W. Dale Harvey
824 Stanford Avenue
Clovis, California 93611
Re: Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-04-097
Dear Mr. Harvey:
This letter is in response to your.request for advice regarding the gift provisions
ofthe Political Reform Act (the "Act').I Because yo* qr.rti,o, "or""*Jpait conduct,
we are treating your letter as a request for informal assistance. The Fair Political practices
Commission (the "Commission') does not provide advice relating to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(bX8XA) - copy enclosed.) However, in circumstances involvingpast
conduct, the Commission will provide a general explanation ofthe requirements of the
Act where the "assistance sought is related to possible amendment ofprevious reports
filed by the person requesting the advice." (Regulation 18329(c)(4XA).) In addition, the
Commission does not act as the finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby
(te7s) 1 FPPC ops. 71.)
QUESTTONS
l. Are you prohibited under the Act from accepting a gift of more than $340 from
your girlfriend?
2. ls a gift from your girlfriend reportable?
3. If the gift is rqrortable, do you need to amend your Form 700 to list the gift
even if it has been returned?
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations apperu at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
€.+'


File No. I-04-097
Page No.2
CONCLUSIONS
1. - 3. Gifts of a personal nafure received in a "bona fide dating relationship" are
not subject to the reporting or gift limitation provisions of the Act.
FACTS
You work for the Cenkal Valley Regional Water Quality Control Board (State
WaterResources Conkol Board) as a seniorwaterresources control engineer. your
jurisdiction is statewide. Yourposition is designated in your agency's ionflict of interest
code and your disclosure category is categoryl.
On October 15,2003, your girlfriend ofovertwo and one-half years gave you a
personal gift of $8,000.00. She is not subject to regulation by your employer. you
returned the gift to herwith interest on May l,Z\M.
ANALYSIS
Gift Disclosure: Among the Act's gift provisions are those requiring the
disclosure of certain gifts. Public officials e,numerated in section S72W and employees
who are designated in an agency's conflict ofinterest code as required by section giZOO,
must disclose certain economic interests, including gifts, on the employee's Form 70e
"Statement of Economic Interests." "Each agency determines which official positions in
its organization are required to be designated in its oonflict ofinterest code, and what
specific level of disclosure is appropriate for each position, pursuant to section 87301.
Persons so designated in the conflict of interest code me'designated employee[sJ,, a term
that includes any 'officer, employeg member, or consultant of an ag"r"y' whose position
involves making or participating in making decisions that have a foieseeable and material
effect on any financial interest. ($ 82019, subd. (c) reg lg70l, suM. (a).) Therefore,
individuals may have different levels of disclosure depending on their [categoryJ
designation in their respective agency's conflict of interest code." (Ortizedvice Letter,
G-03-r06.)
As a category I designated employee, unless an applicable exception applies, you
are required to report gifts valued at $50 or more in the aggregatein a calendar year from
any single source.
Gift Limit Section 89503 also imposes certain limits on the value of any gifts
received. Designated employees may not accept a gift from any single source valued at
more than $340 in the aggregate in a calendar year if the employee would be required to
report the receipt of a gift(s) from that source on his or her statement of economic
interests. ($ 89503(c), reg. 18940.2.)
Again, as a category I designated employee, unless an applicable exception
applies, you may not accept any glft(s) valued at more than $3a0ln the aggregate in a
calendar year from any single source.


FileNo. I-04-097
Page No. 3
The Act defines "dft" as "anypayment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received ...,'
($ 82028(a)). Section 820280) provides for certain exceptions, ,rmong which are unused
gifts retumed within 30 days ($ 82028(b)(2)) and gifts receivod from certain enumerated
relatives ($ 82028 (bX3).) Additionally, exceptions areprovided in regulations lBg4Z-
18944.
As defined by the Act, the term "giff'excludes gifts fiom an ernployee,s
immediate family and certain specified relatives. ($ 8202S(b)(3); regulati on rc9a2@)(3).)
The definition, however, does not exclude gifts from persons who me in a dating
relationship with the recipient. "Nevertheless, the Commission has advised that personal
gifts received within an established bona fide dating relationship are not gifts rmder the
Act. This tlpe ofrelationship is similar to a familial or spousal relationsfiip, in which
people frequently exchange personal gifts and disclosure or disqualification would not
further the purposes of the Act." (Albuquerque Adice Letter, No. e-oot 2a; DeRosa
Advice Letter, No. I-98-284; Shea Advice Letter, No. A-g4-0g5.)
Therefore, under this rule, personal gifts received in a bona fide dating
relationship are neither reportable nor subject to the gift limit provisions of thi Lct.
If you have any other questions regmding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-566A.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

€.+'
